name: WhiteboxTools Python Frontend (Geospatial Analysis)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); John Lindsay / University of Guelph

performance.notes: Rust-based binaries; multi-threaded; fast for terrain analysis

agent_readiness.notes: Python frontend for WhiteboxTools Rust binary (500+ terrain/geospatial tools). import whitebox. wbt = whitebox.WhiteboxTools(). wbt.hillshade(dem='dem.tif', output='hillshade.tif', azimuth=315, altitude=30). wbt.slope(dem='dem.tif', output='slope.tif'). wbt.flow_accumulation_full_workflow(dem='dem.tif', out_dem='conditioned.tif', out_pntr='d8pntr.tif', out_accum='accum.tif'). wbt.watershed(d8_pntr='pntr.tif', pour_pts='outlets.shp', output='ws.tif'). 500+ tools. Downloads binary on first run.

gotchas: Downloads Rust binary on first import (~50MB) — needs internet at setup
Python is thin wrapper — no numpy/xarray integration, uses file paths
Some tools are only in WhiteboxTools Pro (commercial)
Error messages from Rust binary can be cryptic
No type annotations — poor IDE support
best_when: Terrain analysis (slope, aspect, hillshade, curvature, TPI)
When 500+ geomorphometry tools are needed in one package
avoid_when: Simple terrain metrics where GDAL tools suffice
When WhiteboxTools Pro features are needed without license
summary: WhiteboxTools Python frontend wraps 500+ Rust-based terrain/geospatial tools. wbt.hillshade(), wbt.slope(), wbt.flow_accumulation_full_workflow(), wbt.watershed(). MIT. File-path based API. Downloads Rust binary on first use. Best for terrain analysis (DEM processing), watershed delineation, and LiDAR processing. No xarray/numpy integration. Some tools require WhiteboxTools Pro license.
